claim: isolated evaluation relies on easy-to-evaluate attributes over important ones, reversing when options are directly compared.
high perceived capacity to act morally implies low perceived vulnerability to suffering, creating an inverse relation between agency and patiency.
moral transgressions activate a psychological need for physical cleansing, and engaging in cleansing alleviates moral distress.
ordinary language use reveals specific conditions under which behaviors are attributed as intentional.
elevated power reduces spontaneous perspective-taking and empathy, causing overreliance on one's own vantage point.
bodily experience of holding weight promotes more elaborate cognitive processing and heightens perceived importance of abstract concepts.
mentally activating social-connectedness traits influences physical temperature experience, independent of actual ambient conditions.
people psychologically distance themselves from past experiences and selves inconsistent with current self-image, more strongly under high self-esteem.
establishing nonprejudiced identity through past behavior licenses subsequent expression of prejudiced or immoral attitudes.
mortality-induced biases depend on death-thoughts being accessible yet outside conscious awareness, disappearing under reflective engagement.
stereotypes activate automatically but their behavioral expression depends on perceiver intention and available cognitive resources.
gender information in language automatically influences judgment speed, independent of explicit awareness or stated beliefs.
affirming positive moral identity reduces subsequent altruistic behavior, while threatening it increases moral action as identity restoration.
superstitious beliefs and practices improve performance by boosting perceived self-efficacy and task persistence.
brief experience of physical warmth shapes perception of others as more interpersonally warm and increases prosocial choice.
pairing liked or disliked music with products directly conditions product preferences through classical conditioning, independent of information content.
relationship commitment motivates forgiveness by promoting prorelationship cognition that reinterprets betrayal, overriding vengeance impulses.
muscle-based facial configuration influences emotional but not cognitive responses, supporting embodied origins of affect.
describing action in imperfective aspect (ongoing) versus perfective (completed) increases attributed intentionality by enabling richer action imagery.
psychostimulant-induced dopamine elevation blocks effort-induced regulatory depletion, linking control fatigue to neurotransmitter dynamics.
